entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A hardy cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) cultivated in Africa, India, and Arabia, known for growing in poor soils.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A region where pearl millet is cultivated.

entities.name: ARABIA
entities.description: A region where pearl millet is cultivated.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The region of origin and cultivation of pearl millet.

entities.name: FORAGE
entities.description: A use of pearl millet as animal feed.

entities.name: CEREAL CROP
entities.description: A use of pearl millet for human food.

entities.name: POOREST SOILS
entities.description: Low-quality soils where pearl millet is grown.

entities.name: HARSH CLIMATIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Extreme environmental conditions under which pearl millet is grown.

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Biological factors that negatively affect the yield of pearl millet.

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW
entities.description: A major biotic yield-reducing disease affecting pearl millet.

entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: The causal agent of downy mildew.
